Most creators in Pakistan avoid discussing the real challenges they face when selling digital products online. They're afraid of being seen as incompetent or unprofessional, so they sweep their problems under the rug and keep pushing forward, even though this approach often leads to burnout and financial instability. But the truth is, creating digital products is already a challenging and competitive space, and creators shouldn't have to deal with the added stress of navigating complex payment systems.
Why the Popular Advice Is Wrong

The popular advice for creators in Pakistan is often to use workarounds like cryptocurrency or peer-to-peer payment services. While these solutions might seem like a quick fix, they often come with their own set of problems, such as high fees, limited functionality, and a lack of security. Moreover, they don't address the underlying issue of restrictive banking regulations, which is the root cause of many creators' payment problems.
